description.value: Multi-agent orchestration platform for Claude Code with swarm topologies, hooks-based enforcement, persistent memory, and MCP tooling
description.comment: Positioned as the leading agent orchestration platform for Claude; v3.5 series. The project repo was renamed from claude-flow to ruflo but is still distributed primarily as the `claude-flow` npm package.

repository.comment: Repository was renamed to ruvnet/ruflo; the claude-flow URL still resolves via redirect.

first-release.comment: PulseMCP lists first release June 2, 2025; alpha v2.0.0-alpha.3 in July 2025 introduced MCP auto-setup.

pricing-model.comment: MIT-licensed OSS; users supply their own LLM provider API keys.

covered-phases.comment: CLAUDE.md enforces a Research → Design → Implementation → Testing → Review loop with TDD-London and ADR-driven architecture; the SONA/RJDCR loop (Retrieve → Judge → Distill → Consolidate → Route) covers discovery and consolidation.

methodology-style.comment: Spec-driven with strict phase ordering; multi-agent swarm (queen-led hierarchy, 60+ agent types); primarily a harness around Claude Code; TDD London School is mandated for new code.

opinionated.comment: Enforces strict phase ordering, TDD-first, file size limits (<500 lines), and 'do what is asked; nothing more' behavioral rules.

ceremony-level.comment: Specs, ADRs, tests, hooks, multi-agent coordination and checkpoint gates add substantial process; not as prescribed-heavy as BMAD but well above lightweight.

mechanization-level.comment: Single CLI orchestrates 100+ agents, 17+ hooks, and 12 daemon workers; `npx ruflo@latest init` + `claude mcp add ruflo` wires an end-to-end managed pipeline.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: Post-task hooks require explicit approval; checkpoint verification gates; plan permission mode exists alongside acceptEdits/bypassPermissions.

approval-gates.comment: Spec-driven gates before implementation, plan mode gates edits, post-task hooks gate artifact release. Deploy gating not explicitly prescribed.

autonomous-e2e.comment: bypassPermissions mode plus 12 background daemon workers allow unattended end-to-end runs; daemon workers audit/optimize/consolidate/predict.

autonomy-level.comment: Default configuration still enforces phase gates and post-task approvals, but the framework ships with explicit autonomous modes and background daemons; sits between phase-level (3) and fully autonomous (5).

rollback-support.comment: `migrate` command supports V2→V3 rollback with full recovery; session-restore preserves state mid-workflow.

supported-agents.comment: First-party support for Claude Code (CLAUDE.md) and OpenAI Codex (AGENTS.md); dual-mode orchestration runs both in parallel with shared memory.

editor-integrations.comment: Primary surface is the CLI. MCP server works with Claude Desktop, VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf, ChatGPT, and JetBrains IDEs as clients.

model-agnostic.comment: 3-tier routing across Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Cohere, and Ollama via agentic-flow integration.

mcp-support.comment: Native MCP server exposing 313 tools across 31 modules; `claude mcp add ruflo -- npx ruflo@latest mcp start`.

artifact-formats.comment: Specs and ADRs in markdown; settings and plugin manifests in JSON; memory persisted to SQLite (not a user-facing artifact format).

artifacts-covered.comment: Specs → /docs, code → /src, tests → /tests, config → /config; ADRs tracked (70+ referenced). Release notes and user docs are not first-class pipeline outputs.

project-layout.value: .claude/ (skills, helpers, hooks, settings) and/or .agents/ for Codex; CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md at repo root; /docs, /src, /tests, /config directories

artifacts-in-git.comment: Specs, ADRs, and config live in the repo alongside source code; AgentDB memory is local state but not the primary artifact store.

language-agnostic.comment: No stack-specific assumptions; TDD and phase rules apply generally. Vitest is referenced but not required.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: 17+ pre/post lifecycle hooks, TDD-first test gates, reviewer/security-auditor agents, and post-task approval checkpoints; rules themselves are also prompt-level instructions.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Explicitly enforces Research → Design → Implementation → Testing → Review; hooks block implementations that violate architectural decisions.

artifact-validation.comment: Hooks enforce file-size limits, typed interfaces for public APIs, and ADR-compliance checks before merges; not a JSON-schema validation layer but structural rules are machine-enforced.

memory-implementation.comment: AgentDB v3 hierarchical memory (working/episodic/semantic) + HNSW vector search + PostgreSQL RuVector + SQLite WAL + markdown (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md). Spans vector-db, kv-store, and markdown-files — tagged hybrid.

persistent-memory.comment: Session-restore reloads state across runs; AgentDB embeds prior markdown memory on session start.

project-context-files.comment: Uses CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md; produces .claude/ skills and hooks.

cost-tracking.comment: @claude-flow/integration tracks token deltas per run (ReasoningBank -32%, Agent Booster -15%, cache -10%); no documented hard budget limits surfaced.

run-history.comment: AgentDB records trajectories for reinforcement learning; daemon workers log to audit/document workers.

telemetry-opt.comment: Analytics described as opt-in, configured via environment variables.

custom-templates.comment: Pre-built collaboration pipelines (feature, security, refactor, bugfix) are customizable; Plugin SDK allows user-defined templates.

hooks.comment: 17 lifecycle hooks (pre-edit, post-edit, post-task, intelligence, route, pretrain, teammate-idle, task-completed, etc.) plus 12 daemon workers.

sub-agents.comment: 60+ agent types in default registry; Plugin SDK for custom workers/providers; Agent Teams feature spawns teammates with run_in_background.

marketplace.comment: 20+ plugins distributed via IPFS/Pinata registry, installable with `npx claude-flow@v3alpha plugins install <name>`.

install-mechanism.comment: `npx ruflo@latest init` (published as claude-flow, @claude-flow/cli, ruflo) or curl install.sh from cdn.jsdelivr.net.

setup-time.comment: Single `npx` init auto-configures hooks, MCP, and project files.

self-hosted.comment: Runs entirely locally; cloud Flow Nexus integration is optional.

requires-account.comment: No ruflo/Claude-Flow account required; only LLM provider API keys (which every comparable tool also needs).

git-worktree-support.comment: No explicit git-worktree integration found in README/CLAUDE.md; parallel execution is via in-process agent swarms and shared memory rather than worktrees. Marked null pending clearer documentation.

documentation-quality.comment: Extensive README, CLAUDE.md playbook, wiki, and marketing site; coverage is broad but the rapid release cadence and rename (claude-flow → ruflo) create some drift between docs and current behavior.

github-stars.comment: 32.6k stars as listed on the repo page on 2026-04-21; changes rapidly.

active-maintenance.comment: v3.5.80 released 2026-04-11 (10 days before verification); 6,067 commits on main.

example-projects.comment: Ships pre-built collaboration pipelines (feature, security, refactor, bugfix) and wiki quickstart guides; domain plugins (Healthcare/Financial/Legal) are documented use cases.

community-channel.comment: Active Discord (discord.com/invite/dfxmpwkG2D); GitHub issues/wiki used for ongoing discussion.
